Arizona Department of Corrections, Rehabilitation and Reentry (ADCRR)
Primary Agency: Arizona Department of Corrections, Rehabilitation and Reentry (ADCRR)
Notes: The Arizona Department of Corrections, Rehabilitation and Reentry (ADCRR) currently has a 287(g) Jail Enforcement Model (JEM) agreement with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Under this agreement, some state prison guards are trained and authorized by ICE to perform certain immigration enforcement functions within the prison. These include: Screening incarcerated individuals for immigration status; Interviewing individuals suspected of being removable; Preparing immigration charging documents; Issuing ICE detainers and transfer paperwork; and referring detainees for transfer to ICE custody after criminal proceedings. Read more about 287(g) agreements here: https://www.ilrc.org/practitioners/national-map-287g-agreements
